---
title: 'Complain or Complete: Two ways to deal with disappointment.'
date: '2020-03-24T14:09:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '2450594891305522905'
blogger_filename: '/2020/03/complain-or-complete-two-ways-to-deal.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Musings'
---

Nothing about the last few days.... Last few weeks.... Last few months.... Last few years.... Last few decades.... Ah heck. Nothing about anything in my entire life has gone according to plan.<div><br></div><div>So I have a choice.</div><div><br></div><div>* <b>I can complain</b>, make excuses for why I can't succeed.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Bury my head in busy work, distractions, and addiction. Come up for air one day to realize it's been ten years and I missed moments with my kids because I wasn't being present and missed opportunities to do meaningful work because it was "too hard".</div><div><br></div><div>* <b>I can complete</b>... One task. I can do one thing that is important even if something else "feels" more urgent.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I can pause myself from taking a shower and going to bed because my child has decided that 9pm is when he wants to get all ten thousand words out. But I am being present for him and investing in him.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I can make one voice recording. Even if I delete it, I'll have practiced using the software. I may not actually launch the Podcast I intended this week, or even this year, but I can take mini steps.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I may not know how to start a career in Voiceover, but I can watch YouTube videos by voiceover coaches. I can listen to industry Podcasts. I can order one book. Take one class.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I may not have the house I want in the state I want it in. But I can organize one room. Order one item. Hang one picture. I can make one move toward the order it if the chaos.</div><div><br></div><div>I may have been shocked at the abhorrent decision of a friend that severed our friendship. I've actually seen a few of those this last few years. Each time I'm surprised. But... I can take inventory of my own actions, and ask God if there was anything I should have done differently, for next time with new people. I can evaluate the type of people I associate with, and choose healthier individuals, and raise the quality of my friendships to ensure I am building friendships with safe people. I can listen to or read works by people such as <a href="https://youtu.be/Eq2TyMkz0I4">Dr Henry Cloud</a> who will help me determine what makes someone a safe person to invest in. I can go to counseling and allow the excellent Dr Jana to simultaneously show me Grace where I have none for myself, lead me to God's Grace and Vision for me, and yet call me to a higher order of living, challenge me to live a truer, higher, more authentic life before God which becomes a higher life before people.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I can set new boundaries, to prevent people from dumping their toxic waste on my yard. I can love people without letting them into my inner circle. Preserving the inner sanctuary for safe people. Safe people aren't perfect, either, but they're safe.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Today, you can Complain about what is or you can Complete one thing that will be a vote for your new identity and new life.</div><div><br></div><div>#Selah</div><div><br></div><div><a href="https://youtu.be/Eq2TyMkz0I4">How to identify a safe and unsafe people, Dr Cloud</a></div><div><br></div><div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1585058946331419-0-5ba7a5397381.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0"   src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1585058946331419-0-5ba7a5397381.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><br></div>
